mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic slowdown, prompting equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100.0 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134021.0 shows strong employment baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the PAYEMS drop, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing construction activity combined with robust consumer credit expansion indicates synchronized demand-side momentum, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 861.0 and RSAFS at 415695.0 signal demand momentum
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.509 reflects near-trend industrial output
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns such that SPX 60-day forward returns are flat or negative during consecutive quarters of INDPRO expansion, yielding a negative cumulative return attribution.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation readings above the Federal Reserve's implicit target force market participants to anticipate prolonged restrictive policy, which elevates the equity risk premium and suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.025 implies persistent price pressures
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.5% threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.10 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as momentum traders front-run earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show negative or flat growth while SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive, breaking the causal link between real output expansion and short-term equity momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and stagnant housing starts indicates speculative demand replacing structural demand, which increases financial fragility and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 415695.0 vs HOUST at 861.0 shows credit-housing divergence
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict drawdowns, producing positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS in a factor model.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market characterized by rising nonfarm payrolls alongside a stable unemployment rate signals wage-driven productivity gains, which boosts corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.8% with PAYEMS at 134021.0 indicates labor tightness
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds the threshold while unemployment simultaneously spikes, yet SPX 20-day forward returns are negative, indicating the signal misprices labor market deterioration as expansionary.
